source.title: COMPASS for rapid combinatorial optimization of biochemical pathways based on artificial transcription factors

initial_request.research_problem: Achieving balanced expression of multiple genes in synthetic pathways in S. cerevisiae, where optimal expression levels are unknown a priori, and existing combinatorial assembly methods are limited by sequence dependency and E. coli-based assembly difficulties.
initial_request.known_context: Balanced expression of multiple genes is central for establishing new biosynthetic pathways or multiprotein cellular complexes. High-level expression of pathway genes often increases metabolic burden. Golden Gate cloning is limited by sequence dependency and E. coli assembly difficulties.
constraints: Optimal expression levels unknown a priori
Sequence dependency of restriction enzyme-based assembly
Difficulty of assembling large plasmids in E. coli
quantifiable_goals: Rapid combinatorial assembly of up to ten pathway genes
Decouple growth and production phases to reduce metabolic burden

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Balanced expression of multiple genes is difficult due to unknown optimal levels, and high-level expression often increases metabolic burden. Existing Golden Gate cloning methods are limited by sequence dependency and E. coli assembly difficulties.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to achieve balanced expression of multiple pathway genes while minimizing metabolic burden and overcoming limitations of current assembly methods.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Inducible artificial transcription factors (ATFs) can allow conditional expression of genes, decoupling growth and production phases and reducing metabolic burden.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Develop a method using inducible ATFs to control pathway gene expression.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Inducible ATFs allow a conditional expression of genes.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design a cloning system based on this hypothesis.

evidence.quote_or_span: Balanced expression of multiple genes is central for establishing new biosynthetic pathways or multiprotein cellular complexes.
high-level expression of pathway genes often increases metabolic burden
The most significant limitation of RE-based assembly methods is their sequence dependency, as RE IIs recognition sites must be eliminated from all DNA sequences to be assembled (“DNA domestication”).

GG-based methods are entirely based on construction steps performed in E. coli, which often is not straightforward, in particular for assemblies of genes of long pathways; amplification of large plasmids is not easily done in E. coli

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis that inducible ATFs can decouple growth and production.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a cloning system that enables rapid combinatorial assembly of pathways with ATF control.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A three-level cloning system with positive selection and CRISPR/Cas9 integration can efficiently generate combinatorial libraries.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design COMPASS with three-level cloning (Entry, Acceptor, Destination vectors) and positive selection.
evolution_trajectory.observation: COMPASS requires only 11 core vectors to generate libraries of millions of constructs for ten-gene pathways.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test ATF activity in genomic context to validate the design.

evidence.quote_or_span: COMPASS allows the rapid combinatorial assembly of up to ten pathway genes, each transcriptionally controlled by nine inducible ATF/binding site (BS) units
COMPASS makes use of positive selection protocols, which severely reduces the need for checking individual constructs
COMPASS is equipped with multi-locus CRISPR/Cas9-mediated modification capacity

An important aspect of COMPASS is that it only requires 11 core vectors (one Entry, eight Acceptor, and two Destination vectors) to establish combinatorial libraries of thousands to millions of constructs for ten-gene pathways.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: COMPASS design established; need to validate ATF activity in genomic context.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether genomic position affects ATF transcriptional output.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Genomic position may affect ATF activity but relative strengths should be preserved.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Integrate nine ATF/BS regulators into a second locus (XII-5) and quantify transcriptional output.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Genomic position affected transcriptional output, but relative strengths of regulators were preserved.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Proceed to combinatorial assembly of pathway modules.

evidence.quote_or_span: we tested whether the genomic locus into which the nine regulators are integrated affects their transcriptional activity. To this end, we placed them into a second locus, XII-5, and quantified the transcriptional output and noise level.

the genomic position affected the transcriptional output of the plant regulators. However, the expressional effect exerted by the different regulators was well preserved. The strongest regulators always led to the highest expression mean of the reporter protein, regardless of where the regulator was placed in the genome

evolution_trajectory.state_before: ATF activity validated; ready to assemble pathway modules.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether the three-level cloning with positive selection works for pathway assembly.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Combinatorial cloning of ATF/BS-CDS modules into Destination vector I using four rounds of assembly with positive selection will yield correct constructs.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform four rounds of assembly with positive selection on selective media.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Yeast cells with successful constructs grew on selective media, confirming assembly.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Measure growth defect upon induction to assess metabolic burden.

evidence.quote_or_span: Yeast cells with successful constructs grow on SC-Ura/-His medium.
Yeast cells with successful constructs grow on SC-Ura/-His/-Leu medium.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Pathway assembled; need to quantify metabolic burden.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether induction of pathway genes causes growth defect.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Induction will cause a measurable growth defect due to metabolic burden.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure growth rate of strain MXintpDI 1 in inducing vs non-inducing medium.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Strain MXintpDI 1 showed a 5.5% reduction in growth rate in inducing medium compared to WT.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Screen combinatorial libraries for production phenotypes.

evidence.quote_or_span: Compared to WT, strain MXintpDI 1 did not show a growth defect in noninducing medium, while it showed a 5.5% reduction in growth rate in inducing medium

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Growth defect quantified; ready to screen libraries for production.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What fraction of library members produce the target compound?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A significant fraction may be non-productive due to suboptimal expression levels.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Co-engineer β-ionone and naringenin pathways and screen 20 colonies by flow cytometry.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Approximately 30% of library members showed no or low naringenin production.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze genotypes of better producers to identify patterns.

evidence.quote_or_span: Flow cytometry revealed that approximately 30% of the library members show no- or low-NG production (Narion 1–6; Fig. 6c, Supplementary Data 13).
The better producers (Narion 16–20) harbor mostly medium ATF/BS units upstream of NG genes (except Narion 17 and 20, which both contain strong ATF/BS units upstream of AtPAL-2).

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What ATF/BS combinations correlate with better production?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Better producers harbor mostly medium ATF/BS units upstream of naringenin genes.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Sequence ATF/BS units in top producers (Narion 16–20).
evolution_trajectory.observation: Better producers harbor mostly medium ATF/BS units upstream of NG genes, with exceptions.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Note limitations of current COMPASS version.

evidence.quote_or_span: The better producers (Narion 16–20) harbor mostly medium ATF/BS units upstream of NG genes (except Narion 17 and 20, which both contain strong ATF/BS units upstream of AtPAL-2).
Flow cytometry revealed that approximately 30% of the library members show no- or low-NG production (Narion 1–6; Fig. 6c, Supplementary Data 13).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: COMPASS successfully demonstrated for up to 10 genes.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Pathways with more than 10 genes cannot be assembled with current vectors.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Additional plasmids can extend capacity beyond 10 genes.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Include plasmids pCOM001 and pCOM009 in the toolkit to extend capacity.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Current COMPASS version limited to 10 genes; additional plasmids extend capacity.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Summarize design advantage of decoupling growth and production.

evidence.quote_or_span: To extend the capacity of COMPASS even further—for pathways with more than ten genes—we included two additional plasmids (pCOM001 and pCOM009) in the toolkit.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Confirm that COMPASS decouples growth and production phases.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: COMPASS employs modular and inducible transcription factor-based controllers that allow decoupling growth and production phases.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Validate by measuring growth rates after induction.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Growth rates decrease by 7–16% after induction, confirming decoupling.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Conclude that COMPASS is effective for combinatorial pathway optimization.

evidence.quote_or_span: COMPASS employs modular and inducible transcription factor-based controllers that allow decoupling growth and production phases.

metrics: ATF/BS unit strength correlation with production
success_verification.final_conclusion: COMPASS enables rapid combinatorial optimization of pathways by decoupling growth and production using inducible ATFs, with a capacity of up to 10 genes extendable by additional plasmids.
limitations: Current version limited to 10 genes without extension plasmids
Growth defect of 5.5% observed in one strain
